acceptance: A process exit before rename leaves canonical text unchanged and startup reports not_applied.
A process exit immediately after rename leaves canonical text changed, preserves the pre-apply snapshot, and startup reports applied_receipt_missing.
A process exit after the low-level transaction completes but before receipt leaves canonical text changed and startup reports applied_receipt_missing.
A process exit after durable receipt storage is recovered as applied_receipt_present without a false failure.
A canonical hash matching neither beforeHash nor afterHash reports conflict and never overwrites the external edit.
A second Apply after an applied crash window is blocked by current-disk drift and cannot double-apply the change.
The real Electron restart path renders one operationId-only reload action, reloads canonical scene bytes, acknowledges the journal, and performs no network request.
A later normal edit to a scene does not turn an already completed operation into a startup conflict.
Renderer document-open commands cannot overtake startup autosave recovery or last-file bootstrap.
A packet import started while the editor is dirty or autosave is active remains Apply-blocked for that session even if autosave clears the global dirty bit during file intake.

machineChecks.command: node --test exact safe-write and crash-reconciliation contract set
node --test review mutate port, Review surface UI, preload bridge, and workspace query set
node --test canonical Review Packet Electron UI E2E
machineChecks.notes: Passed four sequential repetitions and one concurrent run with the crash-restart Electron E2E.

machineChecks.command: node --test crash reconciliation Electron restart E2E

machineChecks.notes: Repository guards, performance baseline, command namespace, menu artifact, path canon, and execution sequence checks also passed; release candidate drift remained a nonblocking pre-existing warning.
Package manifests are unchanged; production dependency audit is clean.

nonClaims: No cross-scene or project-wide transaction is introduced.
No structural or comment auto-apply is introduced.
No DOCX tracked-change apply is introduced.
No Markdown file-authority expansion is introduced.
No cloud, account, authorization, synchronization, or network integration is introduced.
No dependency or UI framework is added.
Release readiness is not claimed; file authority, Markdown completion, capability evidence, and bounded compatibility remain separate phases.
